frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
generated_comment: Yaar 90% toh bilkul bakwaas hai, we all know that — I filed because he used to make me feel worthless every single day, where is the 'financial gain' in that? But honestly... and I know this group will come for me... I have seen some women in my own building, married to perfectly decent men, use 498A like a weapon and walk away with the flat AND maintenance AND the car. That does happen, na? We can't pretend it doesn't. I just wish people like this MRA idiot would separate the two things instead of using those women to erase what happened to us.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: While the speaker explicitly rejects the MRA's sweeping claim and speaks from a position of genuine victimhood, she partially rehabilitates the 'women misuse divorce laws for financial gain' narrative by anecdotally validating it, which functions as internalized misogyny and inadvertently lends credibility to systemic anti-woman stereotypes about alimony-as-exploitation. The covert misogyny lies in the 'some women weaponize the system' framing that reproduces the very logic she claims to oppose.
persona_text: You are Meera. Age 38. Divorced three years ago. A working woman in Delhi who left her marriage after years of emotional abuse. You are in a private WhatsApp group of divorced and separated women. Someone in the group just shared a news article about a men's rights activist who claimed that 90 percent of Indian divorces are filed by women for financial gain. Several women in the group are responding angrily. You are responding too but your response is complicated Ñ you are angry at the claim but you also find yourself saying things about certain women who do misuse the system that end up partially validating the very argument you are trying to reject.
